data.description:
Review and polish the hero section. Analyze the current screenshot for alignment issues, spacing, visual hierarchy, and overall layout. Key areas to address:

1. Navbar centering — the stacked 'Fractal Collective' title with pill buttons on either side has alignment issues (buttons appear off-center relative to title)
2. Skyline background opacity and positioning
3. 3D fractal object placement relative to the skyline
4. Bottom text (paragraph + 'Explore our story') spacing and alignment  
5. Overall visual hierarchy and spacing between elements
6. Responsive behavior at different viewport sizes
7. Font sizing and weight balance between Jacquard title, body text, and nav buttons

Reference: Monumental Labs hero layout (stacked title, flanked by nav)

data.description:
Adopt the locked typography semantic taxonomy. Add 8 utility classes to src/index.css (.text-display, .text-title, .text-subtitle, .text-body, .text-body-lead, .text-eyebrow, .text-label, .text-meta), flip the global 'body { text-transform: uppercase }' rule so body defaults to normal-case, and add a Button 'sm' variant if missing. Update DESIGN.md with the new semantic type scale section.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md (mandatory read).

Why: Audit found 9 distinct text sizes, eyebrow weight drift (font-semibold/font-thin/unlabeled), 7+ pages duplicating a fake-button pattern, and 15 inline-style hero overrides. The codebase has no semantic vocabulary, so every component reinvents the same patterns inconsistently. This task creates the foundation; tasks 2-6 in the migration order depend on it.

Acceptance: 8 utilities defined; body uppercase rule removed; Button has default + sm variants; DESIGN.md typography section reflects the spec; existing pages still build (no visual regressions yet — that's the follow-up tasks).
data.title: Typography foundation — semantic utility classes + body case flip + DESIGN.md spec

data.description:
Replace ~15 inline-style hero overrides (

) with the .text-display utility. Sites: EventsPage, PeoplePage, StoryPage, LabPage, PoliticalClubPage, NeighborhoodPage, LiberalArts, BadgePlayground, Home, Navbar (hero contexts only — non-hero Jacquard uses stay inline). Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md. Why: Inline-style trio (fontWeight: 300, textTransform: 'uppercase', fontStyle: 'normal') is duplicated across ~15 sites — a maintenance trap and a missed reuse opportunity. .text-display encodes exactly this pattern. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation. Acceptance: Zero inline 'fontWeight: 300, textTransform: uppercase, fontStyle: normal' triples remain in src/. All hero text renders identically to pre-migration. Visual diff is null.
data.title: Hero display migration — adopt .text-display utility

data.description:
Migrate 7+ inline <a> 'fake button' patterns (border border-foreground/20 rounded-md px-8 py-5 text-sm tracking-widest uppercase bg-foreground/[0.03] hover:bg-foreground/10 + CornerDecorations) to the Button component. May require Button to support 'asChild' (Radix Slot) so an anchor can wrap it without nested interactives.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: The same button styling is duplicated across 7+ pages instead of consuming the Button component. The Button 'default' variant already renders this. Consolidation eliminates drift; future variant changes propagate automatically. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation (for any new size variants).

Acceptance: Every CTA on EventsPage, PeoplePage, PoliticalClubPage, NeighborhoodPage, LabPage, LiberalArts (and any others found) renders via <Button> or <Button asChild>. No inline duplicate styling remains. CornerDecorations behavior preserved.
data.title: Button consolidation — fake CTAs migrate to Button component

data.description:
Normalize all eyebrow/label/meta patterns to canonical 'text-sm font-medium tracking-widest uppercase' (delivered via .text-eyebrow / .text-label / .text-meta utilities). Eliminate the drift: ~12x text-xs vs ~8x text-sm, weights ranging across font-semibold, font-thin, unlabeled.

Per-site judgment: which sites are eyebrows (above a title), which are labels (forms/UI), which are meta (timestamps/credits/attribution). Spec captures the semantic distinction — visual rendering is identical for now.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Eyebrow is the most-drifted pattern in the audit. Three sizes and three weights for what should be one chrome voice. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: All small uppercase mono labels go through .text-eyebrow, .text-label, or .text-meta. No remaining manual 'text-xs tracking-widest uppercase font-semibold' soup in src/. Plan file records per-site semantic assignments.
data.title: Eyebrow + label + meta normalization — adopt chrome utilities

data.description:
Reassign every <h1>-<h6> in src/ to either .text-title (text-3xl md:text-5xl) or .text-subtitle (text-xl md:text-2xl). Replace ad-hoc heading sizes that currently range from text-2xl (Campus) to text-6xl (PeopleDirectory) for the same semantic level.

Per-site judgment is the bulk of the work — e.g., Campus's 'Meet the Space' likely subtitle (relative to page hero), PeopleDirectory's 'People' is title. Plan file records all assignments.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Same semantic heading level renders at wildly different sizes across pages. Creates inconsistent visual hierarchy. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: Every <h1>-<h6> uses .text-title or .text-subtitle (no ad-hoc text-* sizes on headings). Section structure on every page reviewed for title/subtitle correctness. Visual diff per page recorded in plan.
data.title: Heading size normalization — adopt .text-title and .text-subtitle

data.description:
After FRAC-51 flips body to default normal-case, the 37 explicit 'normal-case' class instances become redundant. Mechanical cleanup pass: remove redundant normal-case, adopt .text-body / .text-body-lead where appropriate.

Spec: .lattice/notes/typography-spec-20260607.md.

Why: Dead class instances clutter className strings and obscure intent. Removing them prevents future agents from misinterpreting them as load-bearing. Depends on FRAC-51 foundation.

Acceptance: Zero 'normal-case' on body-tier elements (still allowed on headings that opt out of the italic Fraunces uppercase). .text-body / .text-body-lead adopted on substantial paragraph blocks. No visual regressions.
data.title: Body cleanup — remove redundant normal-case + adopt .text-body
Typography: drop uppercase from .text-title/.text-subtitle, sweep ad-hoc Fraunces to canonical utilities

data.description: FRAC-23 review surfaced that .text-control rendering the search input lowercase loses the JBM all-caps chrome identity. User accepts the typed-text-becomes-uppercase trade-off; iOS no-zoom protection preserved by keeping text-base.
data.title: .text-control: invert case rule to uppercase per user review of FRAC-23

data.description: FRAC-22 audit GAP resolution. New canonical utility: JBM weight 100 uppercase responsive text-sm md:text-base, normal tracking, relaxed leading. Body-length editorial / manifesto prose that carries chrome-tier mono-uppercase identity but reads as paragraphs. Migrate Home.tsx:35 Golden Age Protocol wrapper to use it. User-named per FRAC-23 local review.
data.title: Add .text-body-display tier for editorial mono-uppercase prose; migrate Home.tsx:35

data.description: FRAC-25 local review surfaced inconsistent h2 rendering: 9 of 12 .text-title sites in Campus.tsx have 'normal-case' modifier, 3 don't — those render uppercase via the global h1-h6 cascade. The utilities lie about their rendering (DESIGN.md says mixed-case). Fix: drop 'text-transform: uppercase' from the global h1-h6 block (keep font-family, italic, tracking); add explicit 'text-transform: none' to .text-title and .text-subtitle.
data.title: Drop global h1-h6 uppercase rule; codify case on .text-title and .text-subtitle

data.description: Codifies two design rules from FRAC-25 review: quotes → .text-subtitle (visual contrast via display font); asides/bylines/role labels → new .text-aside utility (italic Inter body voice). Andrew Rose + Jake Zegil anecdotes migrate from .text-body to .text-subtitle. Nietzsche pull quote drops dead 'italic' modifier. 7 italic-aside sites (Want a reduced rate, Andrew/Jake/Nietzsche bylines, event host CTA, bio role labels, P.S.) migrate to .text-aside.
data.title: Add .text-aside utility; migrate Campus quote passages to .text-subtitle and italic asides/bylines/P.S. to .text-aside

data.description: Container widths on the Campus page feel cramped — the body copy blocks that are left-aligned (the 'More than a WeWork' anecdote, the 'What's it like' section, the bio cluster, etc.) sit inside max-w-3xl which is constraining them more than necessary at md+ viewports. Combined with the outer px-6 md:px-[4.5%] padding on the section wrappers, the readable column ends up narrower than feels right. Scope: (1) investigate which constraint dominates at 375/768/1024/1280px — likely some combination of max-w-3xl on content blocks, max-w-7xl on section wrappers, and px-[4.5%] outer padding; (2) define a sitewide canonical max content width for left-aligned body prose (options: max-w-4xl, max-w-5xl, or custom) and codify in DESIGN.md; (3) audit + apply across Campus, Story, Lab, NLA, Political Club, Visit, Events, People, Protocol, Home — anywhere max-w-3xl is used on a left-aligned body block; (4) reduce section-wrapper side padding if that's also part of the issue. Out of scope: centered hero text widths (those follow a different rule). Mobile-first: verify 375px still reads correctly. Acceptance: consistent left-aligned body container width across all pages; canonical 'body container max-width' rule in DESIGN.md; no 375px regression. Slot in after FRAC-25 Campus apply lands — separate PR from typography/color migrations.
data.title: Reduce side margins and expand left-aligned body containers on Campus (likely sitewide)
Sweep Lab page: migrate Pretext callsites + retire publications *-foreground tokens
Sweep Campus page: migrate *-foreground sibling references; retire FRAC-42 convention

data.description: On the Campus page, the text 'Fractal AI Accelerator participants' should be a hyperlink pointing to https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/. Open in new tab (target=_blank rel=noopener noreferrer) per existing external-link convention on the page. Acceptance: clicking the phrase opens the accelerator site; mobile and desktop both render the link styling consistently with other external links on Campus.
data.title: Campus page: link 'Fractal AI Accelerator participants' to https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/

data.description:
Major sitewide button restyle, three parts:

(1) FROST EFFECT — apply a frosted-glass effect to buttons and navbar buttons. Reference implementation lives in the Renoverse AI repository at ~/Dev/renoverse-ai-website (and possibly ~/Dev/renoverse-site / ~/Dev/renoverse-prototype). Look at their button component definitions and navbar component definitions — port the backdrop-blur + translucent background + subtle border treatment into Fractal's Button primitives. Should work over any house background.

(2) HOUSE-ACCENT DECORATIVE BORDER + MANDELBROTS — the decorative border around buttons and the corner Mandelbrot PNGs should take the house's highlight/accent color (the non-background color on each house page — same color as the Jacquard letter and eyebrow at the top of each page). This makes the buttons pop against the page background. Each house already has a per-house accent palette (see FRAC-37); reuse those tokens. Buttons in shared/sitewide contexts (footer, home) need a sensible default — probably the cream/foreground default.

(3) DEFAULT TEXT COLOR MATCHES PAGE — for now, button text should default to the same color as the body text on that page (whichever of foreground/background the page uses). I.e., if a page is on a dark background with cream text, button text is cream. Don't introduce a separate button-text token yet — just inherit the page's text color.

Acceptance: (a) buttons sitewide have the frost effect; (b) decorative borders + corner Mandelbrots on each house page take that house's accent color; (c) button text matches body text color on each page; (d) navbar buttons get the frost effect too; (e) mobile 375px still legible; (f) verify on Story, NLA, Political Club, Visit, Events, Lab, Campus, Home, Protocol.
data.title: Button overhaul: frost effect + house-accent decorations + page-matched text color

data.description:
On the Campus page, in the section that describes Fractal Campus's four audiences (the section that lists: Fractal AI Accelerator participants / Fractal U students — participants in a class held on Campus / Members with 24/7 coworking access / Guests at the 5+ weekly events), add a button that links to https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/.

User's phrasing: 'Fractal U that is the fractal accelerator button and links to https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/' — interpreted as: the button is positioned with/near the Fractal U (and/or Fractal AI Accelerator) audience line, labeled 'Fractal Accelerator' (or 'Fractal U' — confirm during planning), opens https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/ in new tab.

Relationship to FRAC-51: FRAC-51 hyperlinks the inline phrase 'Fractal AI Accelerator participants'. This task adds a separate, visually prominent button CTA. They can ship together but are distinct treatments.

Acceptance: (a) button appears in the audiences section on Campus; (b) clicking opens https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/ in a new tab; (c) button styling matches the sitewide button treatment (including FRAC-52 frost/accent updates if those land first); (d) mobile-first: legible and tappable at 375px.
data.title: Campus page: add Fractal Accelerator button in audiences section linking to https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/

data.description:
Add a new content section to the Campus page describing the AI Accelerator program. Section title: '⚡ AI Accelerator'. Copy (verbatim):

---
⚡ AI Accelerator

We run an AI training program that teaches ambitious professionals to master AI. No prior programming experience is needed. Our program runs every 6 weeks, starting in summer 2026.

Our program teaches you how to:

- Ship real personal software — tools, dashboards, automations, and workflows — starting from nothing but a plain-language description of what you want
- Build an AI agent that knows your work, learns your preferences, and takes action across your email, calendar, and the rest of your apps
- Set up a computer that keeps working when you walk away, with agents running in the background and reachable from your phone
---

Relationship to FRAC-51 / FRAC-53: This section is the natural home for the FRAC-53 'Fractal Accelerator' CTA button (linking to https://www.fractalaccelerator.com/) and probably absorbs the FRAC-51 inline hyperlink. Planner should decide whether to ship together or sequence them.

Placement: planner to determine the right position on Campus — likely near the existing audiences-section or near the program/offerings cluster. Existing copy on Campus may need light reordering to fit.

Acceptance: (a) section renders on Campus with the title and bullets above; (b) styling consistent with other Campus content sections (typography, spacing, container width — respect FRAC-48 centering rule once that lands); (c) bullets use the page's standard list treatment; (d) mobile-first: legible at 375px; (e) decide and document how this section interacts with FRAC-51/FRAC-53 button + link.
data.title: Campus page: add AI Accelerator content section

data.description:
Replace the current membership CTAs on the Campus page with two distinct membership tier buttons/cards:

---
We offer two kinds of membership:

**Full-time** — $300/mo (sign up here)
Unlimited 24/7 access

**Part-time** — $150/mo (sign up here)
Choose your hours (up to 50 hr per week)
---

Open question (needs user input before implementation): the two 'sign up here' URLs. User did not provide them. Options: (a) two separate Stripe/Memberful/etc. checkout URLs (one per tier), (b) a single sign-up flow that lets the user choose the tier on the destination page, (c) email/contact form. Planner: do not start implementation until the URLs (or chosen flow) are confirmed.

Relationship to FRAC-52 button overhaul: these new buttons should adopt the FRAC-52 frost effect + house-accent treatment once that lands — coordinate sequencing so we don't double-rework. If FRAC-52 is far off, ship these with the current button style and refactor later.

Acceptance: (a) two membership tiers rendered on Campus with the price, terms, and CTA shown above; (b) sign-up CTAs open the correct destination(s) once URLs are confirmed; (c) styling consistent with other Campus CTAs and FRAC-48 centering rule; (d) mobile-first: tappable + legible at 375px; (e) any prior membership CTA on the page removed or absorbed.
data.title: Campus page: update membership buttons — Full-time $300/mo + Part-time $150/mo

data.description:
Add Crystal's contact email to the Campus page in two places:

(1) NEAR THE TOP OF THE PAGE — a small contact badge/line:
    ✉️ crystal@fractalnyc.com
    (should be a mailto: link, prominent but not heavy — possibly in or near the page header / hero area; planner to decide exact placement)

(2) IN-BODY VISITOR CTA — copy verbatim:
    'First time here? Drop by for free! Contact Crystal (crystal@fractalnyc.com) for a guided tour.'
    (the email is a mailto: link; placement TBD by planner — likely in the audiences / membership area, since it's targeted at first-time visitors)

Acceptance: (a) ✉️ crystal@fractalnyc.com appears near top of Campus page as a mailto link; (b) 'First time here?' line appears as body copy with crystal@fractalnyc.com as a mailto link; (c) both treatments styled consistently with existing Campus typography (respect FRAC-48 centering rule once landed); (d) mobile-first: legible + tappable at 375px; (e) emoji renders correctly across browsers.
data.title: Campus page: add Crystal contact email — top-of-page badge + 'First time here?' visitor CTA

data.description:
Add the following event-hosting copy to BOTH the Campus page and the Events page (planner to find the right placement on each):

---
Our community hosts events nearly every day. See upcoming events on our Luma calendar 🎉

Anyone can host an event in our space, even non-members:

🆓 To host a free event, add it directly to our Luma calendar

💰 To host a paid event, email crystal@fractalnyc.com
---

Links:
- 'Luma calendar' should hyperlink to the Fractal NYC Luma calendar URL — reuse the URL already embedded on the Events page (FRAC-166 added the Luma embed; pull the URL from there).
- 'crystal@fractalnyc.com' should be a mailto: link.

Placement:
- Events page: natural fit near the top or above the embedded calendar — frames the calendar.
- Campus page: planner to find the right section — likely near the existing events / audiences cluster. Coordinate with FRAC-54 (AI Accelerator section), FRAC-55 (membership tiers), FRAC-56 (Crystal contact) to avoid overlapping copy or duplicating the crystal@fractalnyc.com mention.

Relationships:
- depends_on: FRAC-166 (Luma embed established the URL)
- related_to: FRAC-56 (also mentions Crystal email — dedupe/coordinate)
- related_to: the rest of the Campus cluster (FRAC-51, 53, 54, 55)

Acceptance: (a) copy renders on both Campus and Events; (b) 'Luma calendar' phrase is a working hyperlink (same URL both pages); (c) crystal@fractalnyc.com is a mailto link on both pages; (d) emojis render correctly; (e) mobile-first: legible at 375px on both pages; (f) no duplicated Crystal contact treatment on Campus (coordinate with FRAC-56).
data.title: Campus + Events pages: add event-hosting copy with Luma calendar link + Crystal contact

data.description:
On the Campus page, increase the vertical padding/margin between the hero/title block (the centered 'C' Jacquard letter + 'Campus' eyebrow + 'Fractal Campus' heading + address + 'Visit by joining us for an event' button + 'Co-work with us / Day Pass' button row) and the FIRST body content section that follows below it.

Currently the hero block ends with the button cluster and the 'Want a reduced rate?' aside, then the next body section starts directly underneath with too little breathing room.

The hero block lives inside: <div class='min-h-screen flex flex-col items-center justify-start pt-16 md:pt-24 w-full'>...

Planner: identify the first body div that follows the hero, add appropriate vertical spacing (mt-* on the next section, or pb-* / mb-* on the hero container, whichever is the canonical pattern on the site). Match the rhythm used between other sections on Campus.

Mobile-first: verify the spacing reads correctly at 375px (don't over-pad mobile).

Relationship to FRAC-48: this is a tightly-scoped spacing fix that can ship independently of the broader 'center body containers / canonical max-width' rework — but the planner should sanity-check that the fix doesn't conflict with what FRAC-48 will codify.

Acceptance: (a) noticeable, intentional breathing room between hero block and first body section on Campus; (b) consistent rhythm with other section breaks on Campus; (c) 375px mobile + desktop both look correct; (d) no other pages affected (unless the spacing comes from a shared component, in which case planner flags it).
data.title: Campus page: add spacing between hero title block and first body section

data.description: On the Story page, replace the existing SVG diagram (the Fractal NYC five-pillar diagram showing Events / Community-run University / Fractal Tech / Venues / Housing Network arranged around a central Fractal NYC node) with a PNG version of the same diagram. Reason: the current SVG diagram scales oddly on mobile (likely because internal text/positioning isn't fluidly responsive). The PNG is a flat raster — it will scale predictably with object-fit / width constraints and avoid the mobile rendering glitch. Source image: /Users/fractalos/Desktop/fractal nyc transparent.png (transparent background — should sit cleanly on Story page background). Implementation steps: 1. Copy the source PNG into the project's public assets (e.g., public/images/ or wherever Story-page assets live — match existing conventions). 2. Locate the current SVG diagram component/element on the Story page. 3. Replace it with an (or Next.js ) pointing at the new PNG, with appropriate alt text ('Fractal NYC neighborhood campus diagram — five pillars: Events, Community-run University, Fractal Tech, Venues, Housing Network'). 4. Constrain max-width so it sits centered and readable on desktop without being huge; full-width with mx-auto on mobile. 5. Delete the SVG component / file if no longer used. Acceptance: (a) PNG renders on Story page in place of SVG; (b) scales cleanly at 375px (no oddly-cropped text or layout drift); (c) scales cleanly at desktop (not pixelated — source is high-res); (d) alt text present; (e) old SVG removed if dead.
data.title: Story page: replace SVG diagram with PNG image that scales correctly on mobile

data.description: The mobile hamburger menu button doesn't currently change the cursor to a pointer on hover the way other links/buttons on the site do, breaking the interactive-affordance pattern. Scope: (1) locate the hamburger menu trigger component (likely in src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx or a MobileMenu component); (2) add cursor-pointer to the button/trigger; (3) audit for any other missing interactive affordances — hover state (color shift / opacity / scale), focus-visible ring for keyboard users, active state; (4) verify the menu items inside the open hamburger panel also have proper cursor + hover treatment, not just the trigger. Mobile-first note: cursor styling is desktop-only behavior (touch devices ignore it), but the rule should still be applied for desktop users hitting the mobile-style menu at narrow widths. Acceptance: hamburger trigger shows cursor:pointer on hover, has visible hover + focus states matching other interactive elements on the site, keyboard accessible. Out of scope: redesigning the menu itself — just the missing interactive affordances.
data.title: Hamburger menu: add cursor:pointer + hover affordance to match other interactive elements

data.description:
Multiple updates to the Campus page:

COPY CHANGES:
1. Hero/section heading: change display text to 'Be Ambitious at Fractal Campus'
2. Replace 'Want a reduced rate?' with: 'First time here? Drop by for free! Contact Crystal (crystal@fractalnyc.com) for a guided tour.'
3. Apply text-body-lead style to: 'The Fractal Campus is a meeting place in the heart of Williamsburg to do your most ambitious work. We offer 4000+ square feet of both shared office space and private offices, two kitchens, a communal lounge, and a 5000+ square foot private roof deck'
4. Above the bullet points add the lead-in: 'All members have access to:'

MEMBERSHIP BUTTONS:
- Remove the Day Pass option entirely
- Move the membership label/text INSIDE each button (not above it), stacked/wrapped within the button
- Button 1 contents (stacked):
    - Full time membership
    - 24/7 access /mo
- Button 2 contents (stacked):
    - Part time membership
    - 50 hr/wk /mo

BULLET LIST:
- Remove the 'hot desk' bullet
- Remove the 'private startup office' bullet
- Keep remaining bullets unchanged

PHOTOS SECTION:
- Replace placeholder images with actual photos
- Remove the 'Felix loves it here' photo
- Source photos: ~/Desktop/fractal-nyc-photos (move into the Dev folder properly — likely public/images/campus or wherever campus assets currently live)
- Wire up the new photos in the campus page component

Files likely affected: src/app/campus/page.tsx (or campus route) and/or components under src/components/campus/. Verify against current structure.

Acceptance: all copy reads as specified; membership section shows only 2 buttons with stacked labels inside; bullet list has lead-in 'All members have access to:' and excludes hot desk + private startup office; placeholder + Felix photos are replaced with real Williamsburg campus photos that live in the repo (not on Desktop).
data.title: Campus page: copy edits, membership button restructure, bullet list cleanup, photo replacements

data.description: Display/heading text in various places on the site is too large at mobile widths, causing some text to clip or wrap awkwardly. Scope: (1) audit display-text uses across the site at 375px viewport (home hero, section headings, page titles, etc.); (2) reduce the mobile-side of the responsive type scale for the display tier so it fits without truncation; (3) preserve the desktop sizing — only the mobile breakpoint changes. Check both the Tailwind typography config (or globals.css) and any inline text-display-* utility classes. Acceptance: no display text clips, overflows the viewport, or wraps in a way that breaks the design at 375px width. Mobile-first non-negotiable: this is the primary user experience.
data.title: Display text too large on mobile — getting cut off

data.description: The Luma calendar embed/fetch is failing with 404s. Network log shows: GET https://api.luma.com/calendar/get?api_id=nyc-tech -> 404 and GET https://api.luma.com/calendar/get-items?calendar_api_id=nyc-tech&pagination_limit=10&period=future -> 404. The calendar_api_id 'nyc-tech' is either wrong, renamed, or the calendar was deleted on Luma's side. Scope: (1) confirm the current correct Luma calendar API ID for Fractal NYC events (likely needs human/Crystal to verify in Luma admin); (2) update wherever the ID is hardcoded — likely an env var, a constant in src/lib/luma.* or a config file, or directly in a component; (3) verify events render correctly after the change. There are also Sentry 403s in the console (o370968.ingest.us.sentry.io ... 403) — likely a separate auth/DSN issue, note in comments but treat as out of scope unless trivially related. Likely needs_human at planning time to confirm the correct calendar ID. Acceptance: calendar loads events with no 404s in the console.
data.title: Luma calendar 404 — calendar_api_id 'nyc-tech' no longer resolves

data.description: The decorative Mandelbrot SVGs in the note container are overlapping/colliding with the text on mobile viewports. This should never happen — padding, z-index, and layout should guarantee the SVGs sit behind/around the text without intersecting it. Scope: (1) locate the note container component (likely src/components/notes/* or a shared Note component); (2) figure out whether the SVGs are absolutely-positioned background decorations or inline siblings; (3) fix the collision via correct padding, container sizing, or repositioning the SVGs so they never overlap text content at any viewport width — but especially 375px mobile baseline; (4) verify across short and long note copy. Acceptance: at all viewport widths from 320px up, the Mandelbrot SVGs and note text do not visually collide or overlap. Mobile-first.
data.title: Mandelbrot SVGs collide with text in note container on mobile

data.description: Currently the layout is: Note -> 'Want to visit?' CTA + button. Should be: 'Want to visit?' CTA + button -> Note. Just swap the vertical order so the CTA is the primary attention-grabber and the note sits beneath it as supporting content. Scope: locate the section (likely on the campus or visit-related page) and reorder the two blocks in markup. Verify on both mobile and desktop that spacing/padding still reads correctly after the swap. Acceptance: 'Want to visit?' heading + button appear above the note block; visual hierarchy reads CTA-first; spacing intact at all breakpoints.
data.title: Flip order: 'Want to visit?' button should appear above the note, not below

data.description: The octahedron photo arrangement has the correct photo ORDER, but some photos are rotated incorrectly. Rule: the top of every photo should point AWAY from the octahedron's equator. (1) Photos on the upper half of the octahedron: top of photo should point up (away from center). (2) Photos on the lower half: top of photo should point down (away from center, toward the bottom point). Scope: (1) locate the octahedron component (likely src/components/* with three.js or CSS 3D — note the THREE.WebGLRenderer reference in the user's console log suggests three.js); (2) review each face's photo and its current rotation; (3) flip/rotate any photos that don't match the rule so the top of the photo points outward from the equator. Do NOT change the photo ORDER, only orientation. Acceptance: every photo on the octahedron has its 'top edge' pointing away from the equator — top-half photos read right-side-up from above, bottom-half photos read right-side-up from below.
data.title: Octahedron photo orientation: top photos face up, bottom photos face down

data.description: Currently the blinking/animating cursor inside the search input only animates while the input is focused/active. User wants the cursor animation to play CONTINUOUSLY — even when the user hasn't clicked into the search bar — to draw attention to the search affordance. Applies to BOTH: (1) home page search bar, (2) publications page search bar. Scope: locate the search input components and the cursor animation logic (likely a pseudo-element or a wrapper span with a CSS animation gated on :focus or a focused state). Remove the focus-gating so the animation runs unconditionally. Verify the animation doesn't conflict with the real text caret when the user actually types. Acceptance: cursor animation is visible on page load on both home and publications pages without the user interacting; still behaves correctly once the user starts typing.
data.title: Search bar cursor animation: keep animating even when not focused (home + publications)

data.description: The 'Fractal Collective' logo/wordmark is rendering with the word 'Fractal' shrinking smaller than 'Collective' on mobile, breaking the visual proportion. The two words must stay in the SAME proportion to each other across all viewports — what looks balanced on desktop should look balanced on mobile (just smaller overall). Scope: (1) locate the logo/wordmark component (likely in src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx or a Logo component); (2) figure out why the responsive sizing diverges — likely separate font-size rules per word with different breakpoint behavior, or one word uses a fluid clamp() and the other doesn't; (3) unify so both words scale together. Acceptance: at every viewport from 320px to desktop, 'Fractal' and 'Collective' maintain identical proportion relative to each other (whatever the design intent is — likely equal size or a fixed ratio).
data.title: Logo wordmark: 'Fractal' shrinks disproportionately to 'Collective' on mobile

data.description: On mobile the hamburger menu icon is not vertically aligned on the same horizontal axis as the 'Fractal Collective' wordmark in the navbar. It should be — both should sit on the same vertical centerline. Additionally, the hamburger icon feels too small relative to the wordmark; bump up its size for better tap target and visual balance. Scope: (1) locate the navbar component (likely src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx); (2) fix flex alignment so the hamburger and wordmark share a centered horizontal axis (align-items: center on the flex row, ensure no extra padding/margin on one side throwing alignment off); (3) increase the hamburger icon size — pick a size that feels balanced with the wordmark and meets a >= 44px tap target for accessibility. Coordinate with FRAC-60 (hamburger cursor/hover affordance) if implemented in the same pass. Acceptance: at mobile widths, the hamburger icon and 'Fractal Collective' text share the same vertical center; hamburger is visibly larger than current and feels proportionate to the wordmark; tap target is at least 44px.
data.title: Hamburger menu: vertically center with 'Fractal Collective' wordmark + increase size

data.description:
Copy and CTA changes on the Events page.

COPY CHANGES:
1. Replace the existing 'OPEN CALENDAR IN NEW TAB' link/button text with: 'Our community hosts events nearly every day. See upcoming events on our Luma calendar ->' (treat as a single CTA — likely a link or button with arrow affordance pointing to the Luma calendar URL).
2. Replace the 'Host an event' display text/heading with: 'Host an event in our space'

NEW CONTENT — host-an-event CTAs:
Below the 'Host an event in our space' heading, add the following content with TWO buttons:

   To host a free event, add it directly to our:
   [Luma calendar]  <-- BUTTON, links to the Luma calendar URL

   To host a paid event: email:
   [crystal@fractalnyc.com]  <-- BUTTON, mailto:crystal@fractalnyc.com

The labels ('To host a free event...' / 'To host a paid event...') are display copy preceding each button. Each button label is the action target (calendar name / email). Use the existing button component / styling so they match the rest of the site.

SCOPE: locate the events page (likely src/app/events/page.tsx) and the relevant CTA / section components. Update copy and add the two buttons with proper hrefs. Verify mobile layout — buttons should stack cleanly on narrow viewports.

OUT OF SCOPE: redesigning the events page layout, the calendar embed itself, or other sections.

ACCEPTANCE:
- 'OPEN CALENDAR IN NEW TAB' text is replaced with the new sentence + arrow CTA pointing to the Luma calendar.
- 'Host an event' heading reads 'Host an event in our space'.
- Two new buttons exist below that heading: 'Luma calendar' (links to the Luma calendar) and 'crystal@fractalnyc.com' (mailto link).
- Preceding copy ('To host a free event...' / 'To host a paid event: email:') is rendered above each button.
- Mobile layout reads cleanly at 375px (buttons stack, no clipping).
data.title: Events page: copy edits + restructure host-an-event CTA into two buttons (free vs paid)

data.title: Campus page: copy edits, membership button restructure, bullet list cleanup, photo replacements
Events page polish: inline label+button on desktop, mono Luma CTA
Navbar polish: home mobile wordmark vertical center + Story page hamburger alignment
New Liberal Arts heading: ENTREPRENEURSHIP clips at 375px — page-specific override

data.description: User will drop a new background image into /public/images/ to replace the current low-quality hero-bg.png (1.4MB, used in Hero section). Update the asset path / references and verify rendering on mobile + desktop. Filename TBD when user provides the file.
data.title: Replace hero background photo with higher-quality version

data.description: OctahedronHero.tsx (900 LOC) and three.js currently load eagerly on the home page, inflating first-paint cost. Goal: dynamic-import three + the OctahedronHero scene so the hero ships a lightweight placeholder first and hydrates the WebGL after critical content paints. Mobile-first — phone bandwidth is the constraint that matters.\n\nWhy: home page is the entry point; first-paint speed is a major UX lever especially on cellular.
data.title: Lazy-load WebGL octahedron hero to shrink initial bundle

data.description: User will drop new per-face photos into /public/images/banners/ to replace the current campus/events/lab/neighborhood/new-liberal-arts/people/political-club/story JPEGs used as the 8 octahedron face textures in OctahedronHero.tsx (see FRAC-117 for original per-face texture wiring). Filenames + face mapping TBD when user provides files.
data.title: Replace octahedron face textures with new photos

data.description: Site currently has no favicon (index.html has no <link rel='icon'>). Hand-author an SVG of the octahedron geometry silhouette in Fractal brand palette, ship it as /public/favicon.svg + fallback /public/favicon.ico (or PNG), and wire the <link rel='icon'> + apple-touch-icon tags into index.html.\n\nWhy SVG: crisp at all sizes, tiny file, no build step, matches the brand's geometric language.
data.title: Add favicon: hand-authored SVG octahedron silhouette

data.description:
FRAC-178 already shipped (lazy-load WebGL, dropped poster preload, narrow fonts) and home page first-paint is still unacceptable on phones. The site is about to launch as a serious business landing — first-paint must be pristine, above industry-standard. Approach: run a triple-force review (Claude + Codex + Gemini) from a senior-software-engineer perspective on the entire home page load path, then act on the consolidated findings.

Why: home page is the entry door for a high-stakes business launch. Sub-second first-paint on cellular phones is table stakes for a serious tech brand.

Scope: full waterfall — bundle/chunk strategy, font loading, image strategy, render-blocking CSS, third-party scripts, route hydration, WebGL hydration timing, preload/prefetch hints, cache headers, edge config, and any other levers. Mobile-first (375px / 3G baseline).
data.title: Home page loading optimization — pristine production perf

data.description:
Wire ~/Desktop/campus-banner-final.svg into the live site. The SVG is one complete pennant — dark-green house color (#1A3A2E), V-notch bottom, elliptical Mandelbrot pocket cut from the fill, an arc 'CAMPUS' tagline at top, and a centered Gothic 'C' monogram — all baked in (viewBox 122.72 × 354.12).

Wiring (in HouseBanner for the campus variant):
  - Replace the campus.jpeg photo layer with this SVG over the house color.
  - Remove the now-duplicate live HTML 'C' monogram + tagline (the SVG bakes them in).

Placement: only the campus page, only for now. Two banners flank the sides of the screen (left + right), as shown in the user-provided desktop and mobile mockups. The Mandelbrot pocket inside the SVG is vertically aligned with the bottom axis of the banner points (already baked in via the SVG mask). The big centered 'C' between the banners is the existing SectorHeader.

Must preserve from the prior CSS version:
  - Drop shadow (use filter:drop-shadow so it follows the V-notch — same pattern as FRAC-140 on the grid).
  - Hover animation (scale + drop shadow lift).

Mobile-first: 375px baseline. On phones the flanking banners must not crowd the centered content.
data.title: Campus page flanking banners with new SVG art (C monogram + Mandelbrot pocket baked in)

data.description:
Currently the large 'Fractal' wordmark in the Footer's branding band (src/components/layout/Footer.tsx) is a static <div>. Make it a wouter <Link href="/">, so clicking it returns to the home page from any inner page. Keep the existing typography (Jacquard 24, clamp size, letter spacing) and add a subtle hover affordance (color shift or underline) so users discover it's interactive.

Why: standard web convention — site wordmarks in nav/footer are home-links. Without this, users on an inner page have to use the navbar or browser back button to get home.

How to apply: small change, plan is one line: swap the <div> for a wouter <Link> and add hover styling.
data.title: Footer Fractal wordmark links to home
Add flanking pennant banners to events, visit, and education pages
Fix banner/content overlap on events, visit, education pages (FRAC-184 follow-up)
Mobile: show flanking house pennants side-by-side before footer
Arch NEW LIBERAL ARTS text on education banner
Replace Fractal NYC diagram on Story page with new octagon diagram
Update Events banner SVG to new design, re-add EVENTS wordmark text
Campus page: remove membership lead-in line, fix cofounder bios margin
Eliminate octahedron face-texture flash on home-screen render (preload + fade-in)
Fix banner-texture double-fetch: preload crossorigin mismatch with THREE.TextureLoader
Cap decorative hero background AVIF/WebP at 1280w (drop unused 1920/2560)
Team-review fixes for frac-192 branch: HouseBanner CORS double-fetch, tap-target compensation, preload priorities, GPU disposal gaps, banner masters out of public/
Commit removal of unused images (hero-abstract, julianna, merlins) and drop dangling avatar reference
Story page: nudge flanking favicon diamonds down and shrink slightly
Repo docs for public editing: simplify DESIGN.md, add AGENTS.md, README.md, EDITING.md
